DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF JANET HAAS' MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER QUASHING THE NOTICE OF HER DEPOSITION AND STAYING THE DEPOSITION FOR 60 DAYS IS GRANTED, ALL DISCOVERY WILL BE STAYED FOR 60 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. THERE HAS BEEN NO SHOWING BY DEFENDANT THAT THERE IS AN IMMEDIATE NEED FOR DISCOVERY TO GO FORWARD AT THIS TIME AND PLAINTIFF'S SHOWING OF GOOD CAUSE FOR DELAY IS SUFFICIENT TO JUSTIFY THE STAY. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. PAUL RENNE, JUDGE PRO-TEM, CYNTHIA HERBERT, CLERK, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE SET FOR APR-16-2013 IN DEPARTMENT 610 AT 10:30 AM FOR FAILURE TO FILE PROOF OF SERVICE ON DEFENDANT(S) AND OBTAIN ANSWER(S), OR ENTER DEFAULT(S). THE FEB-27-2013 CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT MCCOLE, CHRISTOPHER I. JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 5.0 DAYS
DEFENDANT(S) REJECTED PROPOSED ARBITRATOR JOHN LAWRENCE WININGHAM.

DISCOVERY 302,PLAINTIFF JANET HAAS' MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER AND MODIFYING THE DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE AS HAVING BEEN UNTIMELY SERVED AND FILED ON PLAINTIFF, AND, AS A PRECONDITION TO PLAINTIFF'S MAKING ANOTHER SUCH MOTION, PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 1, 2013, WITH RESPECT TO THESE DISCOVERY MATTERS AND TO SPECIFY THE TIME AND OTHER PARAMETERS OF THE DISCOVERY REQUESTED. CONTRARY TO PLAINTIFF'S ASSERTION, DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL'S NOTICE OF UNAVAILABILITY WAS PERMISSIBLE. WHILE EVERY EFFORT IS TO BE MADE TO ACCOMMODATE PRO PER PLAINTIFFS, THEY TOO MUST FOLLOW STATUTORY PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENTS WHICH PLAINTIFF DID NOT DO HERE. PREVAILING PARTY PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM ADRIAN A. COLLEY, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order And Modifying The Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records
Off Calendar Sep-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances, Plaintiff Janet Hass in Pro Per present. (not contest tentative ruling, only asked questions). No other appearance.

The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF JANET HAAS' Motion For Protective Order And Modifying The Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records is denied without prejudice as having been untimely served and filed on plaintiff, and, as a precondition to plaintiff's making another such motion, plaintiff and defendant are ordered to meet and confer no later than October 1, 2013, with respect to these discovery matters and to specify the time and other parameters of the discovery requested. Contrary to plaintiff's assertion, defendant's counsel's notice of unavailability was permissible. While every effort is to be made to accommodate pro per plaintiffs, they too must follow statutory procedural requirements which plaintiff did not do here. Prevailing party prepare order. Judge Pro Tem Adrian A. Colley, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)

THERE HAS BEEN NO SHOWING BY DEFENDANT THAT THERE IS AN IMMEDIATE NEED FOR DISCOVERY TO GO FORWARD AT THIS TIME AND PLAINTIFF'S SHOWING OF GOOD CAUSE FOR DELAY IS SUFFICIENT TO JUSTIFY THE STAY. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Paul Renne, Judge Pro-Tem, Cynthia Herbert, Clerk, Not Reported. =(302/JPT)
